title: Google Maps API Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and snippets for using Google Maps API effectively.

commands.description: Include Google Maps JavaScript API in your HTML.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to embed a Google Map on your webpage.

commands.command: var map = new google.maps.Map(document.getElementById('map'), { center: {lat: -34.397, lng: 150.644}, zoom: 8 });
commands.description: Initialize a Google Map centered at specific coordinates.
commands.scenario: Use to display a map centered on a specific location.

commands.command: var marker = new google.maps.Marker({ position: {lat: -34.397, lng: 150.644}, map: map, title: 'Hello World!' });
commands.description: Add a marker to a Google Map at specified coordinates.
commands.scenario: Use to mark a specific location on the map.

commands.command: var geocoder = new google.maps.Geocoder(); geocoder.geocode({'address': 'Sydney, AU'}, function(results, status) { if (status === 'OK') { map.setCenter(results[0].geometry.location); } });
commands.description: Geocode an address to get its coordinates and center the map there.
commands.scenario: Use to find and display a location based on an address.

commands.command: var directionsService = new google.maps.DirectionsService(); var directionsRenderer = new google.maps.DirectionsRenderer(); directionsRenderer.setMap(map);
commands.description: Set up directions service and renderer on a map.
commands.scenario: Use to display routes and directions on a map.

commands.command: directionsService.route({ origin: 'Chicago, IL', destination: 'Los Angeles, CA', travelMode: 'DRIVING' }, function(response, status) { if (status === 'OK') { directionsRenderer.setDirections(response); } });
commands.description: Request and display driving directions between two locations.
commands.scenario: Use to calculate and show a driving route on the map.

commands.description: Add an autocomplete feature to an input field for place searches.
commands.scenario: Use to enhance user input with location suggestions.

commands.command: var streetViewService = new google.maps.StreetViewService(); streetViewService.getPanorama({location: {lat: 37.869085, lng: -122.254775}, radius: 50}, processSVData);
commands.description: Retrieve Street View panorama data for a given location.
commands.scenario: Use to display Street View imagery for a location.

commands.description: Add a traffic layer to a Google Map to show real-time traffic.
commands.scenario: Use to display current traffic conditions on the map.

commands.command: var heatmap = new google.maps.visualization.HeatmapLayer({ data: getPoints(), map: map });
commands.description: Create a heatmap layer on a Google Map using data points.
commands.scenario: Use to visualize data density on a map.

commands.command: var circle = new google.maps.Circle({ strokeColor: '#FF0000', strokeOpacity: 0.8, strokeWeight: 2, fillColor: '#FF0000', fillOpacity: 0.35, map: map, center: {lat: -34.397, lng: 150.644}, radius: 10000 });
commands.description: Draw a circle overlay on a Google Map.
commands.scenario: Use to highlight a circular area on the map.

commands.description: Apply custom styles to a Google Map.
commands.scenario: Use to change the appearance of map features.
